messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Agent host in editor with no workspace open no-ops
ISSUE BODY: 1. Open VS Code without a workspace
2. Select Agent host
3. Send a message
4. (it no-ops)

--- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) ---
--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## End to End Testing  
You can run `node ./scripts/code-agent-host.js` to start an agent host. If you pass `--enable-mock-agent`, then the `ScriptedMockAgent` will be used.  
By default this will listen on `ws://127.0.0.1:8081`. You can then use the `ahp-websocket` client, when available, to connect to and communicate with it.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\.github\instructions\ai-customization.instructions.md =====  
--- description: Architecture documentation for VS Code AI Customization view. Use when working in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization` applyTo: 'src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization/**'
---

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Agent Host  
The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory.  
If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:///<path/to/agent-host-protocol>/plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### E2E
Workspace skills (project scope): `launch` (drive Code OSS Agents window), `code-oss-logs` (read `agenthost.log`).
- **Scenario:**
1. Put a real customization on disk (e.g. `~/.claude/agents/explore.md` with frontmatter + a body).
2. `launch` the Agents window, authenticate, pick **Claude**, create a session, and **without sending a message** open the customization list.
3. Confirm `explore` appears and **opening it opens the real `~/.claude/agents/explore.md`** (with its body) — not an empty stub.
4. Send a message (materialize). Add a deliberately-broken `~/.claude/agents/broken.md` the SDK won't load; confirm it is **hidden** post-materialize while `explore` remains.
5. Edit `explore.md` on disk; confirm the list refreshes (watcher).
6. `code-oss-logs`: confirm no `[Claude]` customization warnings and the scan ran without the stub bundler.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### Application Management
- Start, stop, and restart VS Code instances
- Open workspaces and folders

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Instructions  
1. **Always read the `sessions` skill first.** This is your primary source of truth for the sessions architecture.
- Invoke `skill: "sessions"`.
2. Focus your work on `src/vs/sessions/`.
3. Avoid making changes to core VS Code files (`src/vs/workbench/`, `src/vs/platform/`, etc.) unless absolutely necessary for the agents window functionality.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\.github\instructions\agentHostTesting.instructions.md =====  
--- description: Architecture documentation for VS Code AI Customization view. Use when working in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization` applyTo: 'src/vs/platform/agentHost/**'
---

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Agent Host messaging fails when VS Code is opened without a workspace",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "workspace", "ui-bug", "e2e"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a VS Code user, I want the Agent Host feature to handle the case when VS Code is opened without a workspace gracefully (either with an appropriate error message or fallback behavior) so that users are informed about the missing context.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given VS Code is opened without a workspace open When selecting 'Agent host' and sending a message Then show an appropriate error message explaining no workspace is configured",
    "Given VS Code is opened with a valid workspace open When selecting 'Agent host' and sending a message Then the agent host should function normally as expected",
    "Given VS Code is opened without a workspace open When attempting to use Agent Host features Then provide clear guidance on how to configure/open a workspace"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Investigate workspace detection logic in agent host protocol implementation",
      "Add workspace validation before message processing in agent host handler",
      "Implement error handling for missing workspace scenario",
      "Update agent host protocol to support graceful degradation"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Add UI feedback/error state when Agent Host requires a workspace",
      "Display helpful message guiding user to open a workspace",
      "Ensure Agent Host option remains accessible even without workspace"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test Agent Host with empty/no workspace scenario",
      "Test Agent Host with valid workspace scenario",
      "Verify error messages are clear and actionable",
      "Regression test existing Agent Host functionality"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed", "Documentation updated"],
  "planning_status": "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is the expected behavior when Agent Host is used without a workspace? Should it show an error, redirect to workspace setup, or provide alternative functionality?",
    "Which specific files/components implement the 'Agent host' UI option and message sending logic that needs investigation?",
    "Is there existing workspace detection code in the agent host module that can be leveraged for this fix?",
    "Should this behavior be consistent across all Agent Host features (chat, file operations, etc.) or just messaging?",
    "Are there any related settings or configurations that should be checked when a workspace is missing?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The agent host feature requires some form of workspace context to function properly",
    "There may be existing code paths for handling edge cases in the agent host implementation",
    "The issue is reproducible consistently when VS Code is launched without any workspace open"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent Host Protocol documentation",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - E2E Testing scenarios",
    "D:\\VSss-codedata\\vscode\\.github\\instructions\\agentHostTesting.instructions.md"
  ]
}
```
